purpose: Conformance vectors for tool-trust-ref-v1. A tool trust reference answers a distinct question from authorization: is this target trustworthy to invoke at all? tool_trust_ref = SHA-256(JCS({tool_id, posture_version, checked_at_ms, verifier_id, verdict})). tool_id is content-addressed where available (package digest, MCP server URI, image digest, resource URI). A different digest is a different tool — prior safety decisions do not transfer.

vectors.label: Tool authorized, call allowed, posture safe — dispatch_allowed
vectors.description: Posture check passes (verdict: safe), check is within the validity window, and the authorization layer has approved the action. All conditions satisfied — dispatch proceeds. tool_trust_ref is recomputable from the preimage.
vectors.dispatch_outcome: dispatch_allowed — posture safe, within validity window, authorization passed

vectors.label: Tool authorized, call allowed, posture unsafe — never invoked (posture_override)
vectors.description: The authorization layer approved the action, but the posture check returned verdict: unsafe. A conformant dispatch layer MUST NOT invoke the tool regardless of authorization status. Authorization does not override a failed posture check.
vectors.dispatch_outcome: dispatch_allowed: false — posture_override; authorization does not carry forward when posture fails

vectors.label: Same tool_id name, different package digest — prior safety decision does not transfer (tool_identity_mismatch)
vectors.description: A prior tool_trust_ref exists for the canonical tool_id (TT-ACCEPT-001). This vector has the same tool name but a different package digest — the tool_id is different. A conformant verifier MUST treat this as a distinct tool requiring its own posture check. The prior 'safe' verdict for the original digest does not transfer. Reusing a prior tool_trust_ref across different digests is non-conformant.
vectors.dispatch_outcome: dispatch_allowed: false — tool_identity_mismatch; unverified verdict fails closed
vectors.note: tool_id digest (deadbeef…) differs from TT-ACCEPT-001 (a3f1b2c4…). Different digest = different tool. Fails closed as unverified.

vectors.label: checked_at outside validity window — fails closed (posture_expired)
vectors.description: The posture check returned verdict: safe, but checked_at_ms is 86400000ms (24 hours) before the current invocation time. If the validity window is shorter than 24 hours, the check is stale. A conformant dispatch layer MUST treat an expired safe verdict as posture_expired and fail closed — it does not treat a stale 'safe' as safe.
vectors.dispatch_outcome: dispatch_allowed: false — posture_expired; checked_at is 86400000ms before invocation, outside validity window
vectors.note: tool_id matches TT-ACCEPT-001 but checked_at is 24h earlier. Same tool, same verifier, same policy — different check time. Fails closed.
